THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND STRICKEN FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PRODUCED WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF NO DOCUMENTS EXIST, DEFENDANT SHALL COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 2031.230. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ADRIANA HIX, AND, LARRY HIX MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER SFAMG SHOULD BE VICARIOUSLY LIABLE FOR DR. JAIN'S MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE, IF ANY. PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT AND STRINGER DECLARATION STATE THE PARTNERS OF SFAMG ARE MEDICAL CORPORATIONS, NOT INDIVIDUALS. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER SFAMG IS VICARIOUSLY LIABLE FOR ANY NEGLIGENCE OF DEFENDANT JAIN AS AN EMPLOYEE OF A PARTNER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action Person With Disability
Off Calendar Dec-29-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Compromise of Person With A Disability hearing this day.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Vantree, CSR: L. Gillian # 12355.

Kenneth M Sigelman Esq. for Petitioner(s): Adriana Nix.
John Ring, Esq. for defendant San Francisco Anesthesia Medical group.
Attorneys are placed under oath and testify.
After hearing the testimony and considering of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Attorney's fees: $ 236,059.70
Medical expenses reimbursement total $ 125,000.00 and $ 50,000.00 (medical expenses)
Reimbursement of litigation costs and expenses total 94,990.88.
A net balance of 743,949.42.

Shall be invested in a structured settlement annuity, subject to withdrawal only upon Order of the Court, or until the Minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Order signed by Judge Donald J. Sullivan

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS ADRIANA HIX, and, LARRY HIX MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Penelope A. Phillips, Esq. of Kenneth M. Sigelman & Assoc. (619( 238-3813 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiff and moving party; Robert T. Lynch, Esq. of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer (415) 397-2800, appearing for Defendants SFAMC; Greg Cavallo, Esq. of Shopoff & Cavallo, (415) 984-1975 appearing for Defendants SFAMC. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether SFAMG should be vicariously liable for Dr. Jain's medical negligence, if any. Partnership agreement and Stringer declaration state the partners of SFAMG are medical corporations, not individuals. Triable issue of fact raised whether SFAMG is vicariously liable for any negligence of Defendant Jain as an employee of a partner. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600

Mo For A Protective Order And For An Order Compelling Robert James Kensic, M.D. To Respond To Deposition Questions
Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Veronica H. Garcia, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: San Francisco Anesthesia Medical Group
Penelope A. Phillips, Esq., appearing by telephone, for plaintiff and opposing party: Andriana Hix and Larry Hix
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff Adiana Hixand Larry Hix's Motion for protective order and for order compelling Robert James Kensic, M.D. to respond to deposition questions granted in part, denied in part:
1. The deponent Robert James Kensic, M.D. shall appear for the resumption and completion of his deposition on a date and time to be agreed by the parties.
2. At deposition, the deponent shall respond to the question set forth at page 23: lines 9-11 of the deposition transcript; and any other questions remaining following suspension of the deposition of March 13, 2009;
3. Request for appointment of discovery referee denied;
4. The deposition of Dr. Kensic may be held in Department 610 on any available afternoon, commencing at 1:30 p.m., and continuing until 4:30 p.m.
5. Request for sanctions denied.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Penelope Phillips, Esq., appearing by telephone, for Plaintiff and moving party: Andiana Hix
Elia M. DeLuca, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Sand Francisco Anesthesia Medical Group

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents as to defendant San Francisco Anesthesia Medical Group is granted. Interrogatories 3.2 (d) and 17.1 Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken.
Request for production of documents 2 Granted. The objections, other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privilege are overruled and stricken. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log.
3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 10 Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken Further responses and documents shall be produced within 15 days of notice of this order. If no documents exist, defendant shall comply with CCP Section 2031.230. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
